_comment: Style anchors for cross-session consistency. Each entry pins a PixelLab id whose rendered image is reused as a style_images/reference_image input for later generations in the same family, so new sprites visually match old ones instead of drifting. Update via the create-sprite skill, not by hand, except to fix a stale id.

hero_character.name: Hero Bee-Person (Male)
hero_character.role: CURRENT approved direction — humanoid bee-person, not an insect body. mode=v3, body_type=humanoid, view=low top-down, outline=single color black outline, detail=medium detail. Description recipe: yellow skin, black hair, small branching antler-like antennae, shirt, pants, insect wings, and a large/clearly-visible yellow-and-black striped bee-abdomen tail as the stinger weapon (must be called out as prominent, not a minor detail, or it under-renders). Use this exact recipe for the female variant and any future customization-driven regenerations so they visually match. The tool takes no image reference for characters, so consistency is param/vocabulary-level — for a female counterpart, keep every param identical and only change the description's gendered details.
hero_character.staged_at: Assets/Sprites/HeroBeePerson/ — LIVE on the Player via HeroBeePersonGenerated.asset (Assets/Editor/BuildTools/HeroBeePersonSkinBuilder.cs).
hero_character.confirmed_by_user: yeah i actually like this design (2026-07-08)
hero_character.facing_note: Idle/Hit/Death sourced from this character's south rotation, Run/Attack from west. Reconciled with the game's single-facing+flip system via CharacterAnimator._idleUsesMirroredFacing=false on the Player (new opt-in field, defaults true elsewhere for backward compatibility).

hero_character_insect_REJECTED.name: Hero Bee (insect body, rejected 2026-07-08)
hero_character_insect_REJECTED.role: DEPRECATED — do not use as a style anchor. Kept only as a record of what didn't work: standard mode's skeleton generation always reads as a human in a costume for non-human creatures; v3 mode plus explicit 'not human/person' text got an actual insect, but the user ultimately wanted a humanoid direction instead (bee evolves into a humanoid version of itself), not an insect at all. See ASSET_GENERATION.md §1.1 for the current direction.
hero_character_insect_REJECTED.staged_at: Assets/Sprites/HeroBee/ — art still exists, SpriteLibraryAsset still exists, just no longer used by the Player.
